texto: We march through snow and we march through rain With naught to lose and naught to gain Sun and moon and sun again One day’s dawn is another day’s end We come by day and we go by night We fall and rise again to fight Sword and spear and bow the same One man’s life is another man’s game They fell like lightning and they raged like fire They lit the blaze on the funeral pyre Picked and chose the paths they trod One man’s will is another man’s blood We live forever and we die todayWe’ve seen the end of eternity Time and fate outwit us all One man’s rise is another man’s fall
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sarah Delena White: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “We march through snow and we march through rain With naught to lose and naught to gain Sun and mo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, fantasy, halayda pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
